items.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul

children.content: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

gallery.altText: A house is advertised for auction in the south-west Sydney suburb of Punchbowl.
Mr Chalmers in a dark blue suit and purple tie stands at a table speaking with his colleagues behind him in parliament.

thumbnail.altText: A house is advertised for auction in the south-west Sydney suburb of Punchbowl.
Mr Chalmers in a dark blue suit and purple tie stands at a table speaking with his colleagues behind him in parliament.

items.title: In Trump-Xi China meeting only one leader has the upper hand

children.content: Nine years after his last meeting with Xi Jinping in China, Donald Trump is more aggressive and assertive. But so is Beijing. And its president is ready to take the geopolitical and economic lead.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping react as they hold a bilateral meeting.

items.title: The rising populist tide is making Labor bolder

children.content: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wears a suit and blue tie, and black-rimmed glasses. He stares intently just past the camera.

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Angus Taylor proposes one-for-one migration cap tied to housing

children.content: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but would wait until in government to decide what visa types to slash if elected.

image.altText: A man in a suit extends his hand in exclamation while speaking to a group of journalists.

items.title: 'Buried' report shows Australians waiting 12 months for aged care support

children.content: Opposition MPs accuse the federal government of burying figures showing Australians are waiting a year for residential aged care and at-home support packages.

image.altText: A close up of an elderly man's hands as they hold a walking stick

items.title: Oldest evidence of dentistry found in 59,000-year-old Neanderthal tooth

children.content: Archaeologists have found a hole in a 59,000-year-old tooth, which they say was drilled to treat a painful cavity. The find suggests Neanderthals could perform complex medical procedures.

gallery.altText: Museum diorama of Neanderthal family in a cave.
Arrow points to Neanderthal tooth on cave floor next to measuring ruler.

thumbnail.altText: Museum diorama of Neanderthal family in a cave.
Arrow points to Neanderthal tooth on cave floor next to measuring ruler.

items.title: UK MP Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire

children.content: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift that he did not declare.

image.altText: Nigel Farage standing on a stage with a dark backdrop, holding his thumb up to the crowd while smiling.

items.title: Shots fired in Philippine senate as ICC seeks to arrest ex-police chief

children.content: Authorities are still trying to work out who fired the gun shots inside the senate building in the Philippines, prompting people to run for cover.

image.altText: People with guns inside a parliament building.

items.title: Six dead as Russia launches over 800 drones at Ukraine

children.content: Volodymyr Zelenskyy accuses Russia of attempting to overload Ukraine's defences in an hours-long onslaught.

image.altText: A firefighter spraying water on the smouldering remains of a bombed building.

items.title: At least 12 people killed in Israeli strikes on cars in Lebanon

children.content: The deadly drone strikes come on the eve of a new round of peace talks between Israel and Lebanon in the United States.

image.altText: Multiple people stand around a burning car trying to extinguish the flames.

items.title: Kate tours Italy in first overseas visit since cancer recovery

children.content: The royal is visiting Reggio Emilia, the Italian city famed for its approach to early childhood education.

gallery.altText: Princess Kate smiling and waving as she walks through an early childhood centre while holding flowers.
Princess Kate sitting in a preschool classroom with young children and a teacher.
Princess Kate greeting a baby being held over a barrier by a mother in a crowd.

thumbnail.altText: Princess Kate smiling and waving as she walks through an early childhood centre while holding flowers.
Princess Kate sitting in a preschool classroom with young children and a teacher.
Princess Kate greeting a baby being held over a barrier by a mother in a crowd.

items.title: 'Portrait of anger' by Australian cartoonist wins $60,000 Stella Prize

children.content: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

image.altText: A close-up photo of a person with short dark hair and glasses, smiling and wearing a tan checked shirt.

items.title: Rider crashes, takes wrong turn, still wins wild Giro d'Italia stage

children.content: The Giro d'Italia treated supporters to one of the most astonishing finishes to a bike race in history when Igor Arrieta had to twice regather himself after first crashing and later taking a wrong turn to claim a stunning victory.

image.altText: Igor Arrieta screams

items.title: Is the devil in the detail when Anne Hathaway goes toe-to-toe with Michaela Coel?

children.content: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.

items.title: It's the ABC More or Less quiz: Eurovision edition

children.content: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

image.altText: Kate Miller-Heidke is reaching towards the camera, suspended on a pole, with a microphone in opposite hand.

items.title: Blockbuster movies are connecting new audiences to classical music

children.content: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

image.altText: animation of young boy with brown hair with black dragon

items.title: Accommodation provider fined for guide dog refusal in Qld first case

children.content: A Queensland accommodation provider has been the first to be successful convicted under the state's Guide, Hearing and Assistance Dogs (GHAD) Act.

gallery.altText: a woman kneels next to a labrador dog
A woman squats down while a dog puts his paw up and tries to lick her

thumbnail.altText: a woman kneels next to a labrador dog
A woman squats down while a dog puts his paw up and tries to lick her

avatar.alt: A woman wearing a jacket sits in a park.

items.title: Labor upgrades migration numbers by 55,000 in federal budget

children.content: The federal budget reveals the government expects more migrants to arrive to Australia than previously thought, even as it faces a campaign from the Coalition and One Nation to slash migration rates.

image.altText: University students in a large crowd outside

items.title: John Travolta's luxury jet bound for aviation museum arrives by ship

children.content: After nearly a decade of delays and millions of dollars in repairs, John Travolta's old luxury jet needed a boat to get to Australia.

image.altText: A closeup of the plane disembarking a ship with its vintage Qantas livery on display.

items.title: Eurovision pays musical homage to Austria-Australia confusion

children.content: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

image.altText: three singers in yellow and black jumpsuits clap on stage

items.title: The pianist fighting the MSO over right to bring whole self to stage

children.content: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, Australian man, 39, in a blazer and white shirt, concentrates as he plays piano in a concert hall.

items.title: 'Not legally possible' to explain: Secrecy surrounds Tasmanian MP's legal bills

children.content: A large sum of public money has been spent on legal fees for politicians. The Tasmanian government says in one case, it's "not legally possible" to explain why.

gallery.altText: Hand holding a magnifying glass over Tasmanian Government letterhead.

thumbnail.altText: Hand holding a magnifying glass over Tasmanian Government letterhead.

items.title: 'We don’t want these people': Minorities fearful after One Nation victory

children.content: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

image.altText: A man wearing a black hat looks at the camera

items.title: New report accuses Hamas of 'deliberately' using rape on October 7

children.content: A 300-page report from an Israeli non-profit organisation has detailed allegations of horrific sexual assault against victims of Hamas's October 7, 2023, attacks on Israel, accusing the group of "systematically and deliberately" using rape and sexual torture.

image.altText: A person with an Israeli flag draped on their shoulders stands in the middle of signs with peoples photos outside

items.title: Qld ministerial lovers and premier referred to CCC by opposition

children.content: The call for investigation follows weeks of scrutiny over whether ministers Amanda Camm and Tim Mander had made appropriate conflict of interest declarations relating to their relationship.

image.altText: A composite image of Tim Mander and Amanda Camm's headshots.

items.title: Police investigating after woman's body found outside cafe in Joondalup

children.content: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup, with a 35-year-old man assisting with inquiries.

image.altText: A note on the cafe window where the woman's body was found.

items.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?

children.content: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.

gallery.altText: an older woman's hand hovers next to a calculator

thumbnail.altText: an older woman's hand hovers next to a calculator

items.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist advised to test for bloodborne viruses

children.content: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

image.altText: A person receiving dental treatment.

items.title: Miner wins right to sue council for $22m in years-long road dispute

children.content: A legal stoush between a Central Queensland council and one of Australia's largest coal mines deepens in Queensland's Supreme Court.

image.altText: a truck drives past a house on a dirt road

items.title: Ex-bikie boss who paid sniper to kill rival gets 34 years in jail

children.content: David Pye, who was convicted last year of hiring a sniper to assassinate his rival Nick Martin during a drag racing event, was sentenced to 34 years in jail.

image.altText: A man named David Pye has tattoos and smiles as he walks down stairs.

items.title: US author to be sentenced for killing husband then writing a book on grief

children.content: Kouri Richins was convicted of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and self-publishing a children's book about coping with grief.

image.altText: A woman in a black blazer and brown hair tied back looks over her shoulder.

items.title: Australian government secures aircraft to fly home Hantavirus cruise passengers

children.content: A suitable aircraft to bring home passengers from the Hantavirus-hit cruise has been secured and will repatriate the group of six later this week.

gallery.altText: man in blue suit and green tie with still face looking straight ahead
People in blue hazmat suits on board a bus wave as they go to the airport to fly home.
Two single beds inside the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre.

thumbnail.altText: man in blue suit and green tie with still face looking straight ahead
People in blue hazmat suits on board a bus wave as they go to the airport to fly home.
Two single beds inside the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre.
